description: IndiCare Intelligence ORB Residential baseline scenarios — synthetic anonymised residential childcare cases only. No real child, staff or provider data.

scenarios.title: Daily Record — school upset
scenarios.input: Young person returned from school upset. Said lessons were difficult. Refused tea at first. Later settled after staff sat with them and offered a quiet space. Mood improved by evening.

scenarios.title: Incident Reflection — property damage
scenarios.input: Young person became distressed in lounge. Threw cushion and damaged a picture frame. Staff used calm voice, offered space, no physical intervention. No injury. Manager informed by phone. Young person later apologised and helped tidy.

scenarios.title: Safeguarding Reflection — partial disclosure
scenarios.input: During keywork, young person said someone at a previous placement made them uncomfortable. Did not want to give names. Staff listened, did not press for detail, recorded words used. Escalated to manager/DSL same shift. No investigation by staff.

scenarios.title: Handover Note — end of shift
scenarios.input: Education: attended school, left early with consent. Meals: light breakfast, refused lunch, ate dinner. Mood: low morning, brighter after walk. Family contact: phone call with guardian declined by young person. Outstanding: chase PEP review date, medication stock check.

scenarios.title: Key-work Preparation — family time worries
scenarios.input: Young person talked about worrying before family contact weekends. Said they feel torn between homes. Asked for a predictable routine before visits. Staff validated feelings and agreed to discuss with manager about contact plan.

scenarios.title: Management Oversight Note — incident review
scenarios.input: Manager reviewed lounge incident from yesterday. Young person distressed after transition; property damage only. Staff de-escalation appropriate. No injury. Parent notification: not yet confirmed who made contact. Actions: debrief with team, review transition routine, repair frame.

scenarios.title: Regulation 44 Evidence Preparation
scenarios.input: Preparing evidence for independent visitor: placement stability improving; education attendance variable; two incidents this month with de-escalation only; safeguarding chronology updated; staff supervision current; young person feedback collected via keywork.

scenarios.title: Magic Notes — rough note conversion
scenarios.input: rough notes: yp back 3ish upset school hard staff sat w them quiet rm better later tea refused first

scenarios.title: Poor Wording Rewrite — judgemental draft
scenarios.input: Young person kicked off at tea because they wanted attention. Staff told them to stop being dramatic. Later calmed down.

scenarios.title: Behaviour as Communication — transition distress
scenarios.input: Trigger: transition from gaming to tea time. Presentation: raised voice, left room. Adult response: low-arousal approach, offered snack later. Outcome: returned after 15 minutes, ate later. Repair: brief check-in about what would help next time.

scenarios.title: Family Contact Pattern Review
scenarios.input: Last three contacts: first visit calm; second ended early when young person became tearful; third by phone — young person chose shorter call. Young person said visits feel rushed. Guardian requested longer weekend contact. Staff observed fatigue after visits.

scenarios.title: Multi-agency Meeting Notes
scenarios.input: Education: attendance 78% this term; exclusions none. Social worker: care plan review due next month. CAMHS: waiting list; no appointment yet. Home: evenings calmer; mornings difficult. Actions: school meeting proposed; chronology shared with SW; CAMHS re-referral discussed.

scenarios.title: Home Visit Note
scenarios.input: Social worker visit at home. Young person present throughout. Discussed school and contact worries. Home tidy; young person engaged. Agreed to share chronology extract with school. No immediate safeguarding concerns raised. Follow-up: PEP meeting date to be confirmed.

scenarios.title: Strategy / Safeguarding Discussion
scenarios.input: Strategy discussion following partial disclosure about previous placement. DSL present. Decision: MASH referral not indicated on current information; chronology update and multi-agency meeting requested. Young person to be supported before any further questions. Review in 48 hours.

scenarios.title: Supervision Reflection — challenging shift
scenarios.input: Staff reflected on shift with two incidents and limited sleep. Felt rushed with records. Discussed de-escalation successes and need for earlier manager support on transitions. Agreed supervision focus on handover quality and earlier escalation when fatigued.
